text: The male patient is positioned in a prone jackknife position, revealing a long fistula tract located in the right buttock at the eight o'clock position.
The surgeon thoroughly curates the fistula tract in the right buttock, widening the external opening to improve access and visibility.
A tube of measured length is inserted into the fistula tract in the right buttock at the eight o'clock position. The tube remains unsecured, not sutured to the surrounding skin.

In the post-operative care phase, the surgeon removes the tube from the fistula tract before each dressing. The tract is cleaned using a cotton swab mounted on an artery forceps, and the tube is reinserted after cleaning.
As the deeper tract starts healing the length of the tube can be shortened gradually. After the deeper tract heals the tube insertion can be stopped.
